summary: The agent concludes that amblyopia affects the eye because it results from abnormal visual input originating in the eye, impairing its functional role in transmitting images to the brain. Despite lack of direct anatomical pathology, the condition depends on normal ocular function for signal generation, thus impacting the eye’s physiological performance.
